questions.prompt: Complete the sentence: "I will call you as soon as ___"
questions.answer: I get the results.

questions.prompt: Complete the sentence: "Before she starts university, ___"
questions.answer: she is going to get a part-time job.

questions.prompt: Complete the sentence: "He won't stop working until ___"
questions.answer: he finishes the project.

questions.prompt: Complete the sentence: "After you take the exam, ___"
questions.answer: you will feel much better.

questions.prompt: Complete the sentence: "We will leave when ___"
questions.answer: everyone is ready.

questions.prompt: I'll text you as soon as I _____ (get) home.
questions.answer: get — No 'will' in the time clause — use present simple: 'as soon as I get home.'

questions.prompt: She won't go out before she _____ (finish) her homework.
questions.answer: finishes — No 'will' in the time clause — use present simple: 'before she finishes.'

questions.prompt: After he _____ (graduate), he is going to travel around Asia.
questions.answer: graduates — No 'will' in the time clause — use present simple: 'after he graduates.'

questions.prompt: We will wait here until the bus _____  (arrive).
questions.answer: arrives — No 'will' in the time clause — use present simple: 'until the bus arrives.'

questions.prompt: When she _____ (be) ready, we can leave.
questions.answer: is — No 'will' in the time clause — use present simple: 'when she is ready.'

questions.prompt: Find the mistake: "I will call you when I will get to the station."
questions.answer: No 'will' in the time clause: 'when I get to the station.'

questions.prompt: Find the mistake: "As soon as the results will come out, I'll let you know."
questions.answer: No 'will' in the time clause: 'as soon as the results come out.'

questions.prompt: Find the mistake: "She won't leave the office before she will send the email."
questions.answer: No 'will' in the time clause: 'before she sends the email.'

questions.prompt: Find the mistake: "After he will finish the course, he is going to look for a new job."
questions.answer: No 'will' in the time clause: 'After he finishes the course.'

questions.prompt: Find the mistake: "We are going to celebrate when we will hear the good news."
questions.answer: No 'will' in the time clause: 'when we hear the good news.'
I'll text you as soon as I land.
Before she leaves, she'll say goodbye.
He won't stop until he gets it right.
After the meeting ends, we'll go home.
When you're ready, we will start.
